title: Captivity
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image= |name=Captivity |region=*[[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Grehollo system/Legends|Grehollo system]] *[[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the [[Grehollo system/Legends|Grehollo system]], and therefore Captivity, in grid square K-7, which ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' designates as part of the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]]. |sector=[[Manoe sector/Legends|Manoe sector]] |system=[[Grehollo system/Legends|Grehollo system]]{{WEGCite|story=To Free the Forgotten|book=The Star Wars Rules Companion}} |coordinates=K-7 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate=*Permanent [[Cloud/Legends|cloud]] cover *Storms and occasional [[Water/Legends|rain]] |gravity= |terrain=*Clifs *Jagged rock formations *Towering [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] peaks |water= |interest=*[[Borolol's Claw]] *[[Captain Dedelin's camp]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports=[[Prisoner/Legends|Prisoners]] |exports= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Do we really want to get exiled to a moon named Captivity? It doesn't sound like a lot of fun to me.|A Rebel soldier|}} '''Captivity''' was a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Manoe sector/Legends|Manoe sector]]. It was covered in a layer of [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] and had frequent and violent storms, and the moon's surface featured high [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and jagged rock formations. One of the peaks on Captivity was the [[Borolol's Claw]], which frequently found itself in the low-pressure center of storms and therefore could be used to attempt a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] landing. The hostile conditions of Captivity made it an ideal world for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to use for [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiling]] [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], who were dropped to the moon's surface in [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]]. The individuals exiled on Captivity included the criminal [[Andews Rolo]], who led a [[Rolo's Ruffians|gang]] of other violent criminals, and the former [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Dedelin]], whose smaller group was composed of [[Politics/Legends|political]] prisoners such as [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial senators]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] carried out a [[Mission to Captivity|mission]] to rescue the politically-important exiles of Captivity. Since the Alliance only dared risk a single starship landing on the moon to evacuate the prisoners, a team of Rebel [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] tasked with locating the exiles posed as the [[Rajah|ruler]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Virujansi/Legends|Virujansi]] and [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Royal Court|advisors]]. When the Empire [[Takeover of Virujansi|took over]] the planet, the Rebel team was exiled to Captivity. Arriving on the moon's surface, the Rebels encountered Rolo and his gang and eventually made their way to the [[Captain Dedelin's camp|camp]] of Dedelin's group. There, the Rebel soldiers helped the exiles transport themselves and their possessions to the top of Borolol's Claw for their rendezvous with the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. After defeating a group of Imperial [[TIE/rc starfighter|TIE fighters]], the ''Falcon'' evacuated both the Rebel team and the prisoners off Captivity. ==Description== {{Quote|Then we probably have to climb through a raging storm to get them up to the top of a mountain. It figures that's the only spot on Captivity where a ship ''might'' be able to land.|A Rebel soldier, on the prisoners of Captivity|}} Captivity was a small [[Moon/Legends|moon]] located in the [[Grehollo system/Legends|Grehollo system]], a part of the [[Manoe sector/Legends|Manoe sector]] in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. A permanent layer of thick and swirling [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] and mist covered Captivity, and the moon's weather conditions were violent with frequent raging storms and occasional [[Water/Legends|rain]]. The winds were [[Temperature/Legends|cold]], loud, and abrasive, and they carried grit with them. From the moon's surface, the [[Atmosphere/Legends|sky]] featured streaks of [[Color/Legends|blue]] and brown, and shades of gray, and the [[Standard day/Legends|daylight]] on Captivity was gray in color. Shifting [[Magnetic storm/Legends|magnetic storms]] ravaged Captivity's surface, which was highly irregular and battered and featured cliffs and other jagged formations of rock and towering [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] peaks. The [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]] storms made [[Starship/Legends|starship]] instruments unreliable, and the atmospheric and surface conditions on the moon greatly increased the difficulty of landing a ship there. ==History== ===Preparation for a rescue=== {{Quote|The real Rajah is safe, and we're here to get captured in his place.''"
"''We want to be exiled, not just captured.|Two of the Rebel soldiers on Virujansi|}} Since the [[Time/Legends|time]] the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was still in its infancy, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] used Captivity to [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] who for various reasons could not be [[Death/Legends|executed]], such as [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial senators]]. The hostile conditions of the moon, coupled with frequent [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighter]] patrols from the nearby [[Grehollo station]], made Captivity an ideal [[Prison/Legends|prison]], with starship [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] unwilling to risk landing there in order to mount a rescue of prisoners. [[File:Virujansi-TSWRC.png|thumb|right|200px|In order to get exiled to Captivity, a team of Rebel soldiers posed as the Royal Court of Virujansi.]] During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Rebel Alliance began to suspect that many [[Politics/Legends|political]] prisoners vital to the Rebel cause, including [[Senator/Legends|senators]] and important Rebel sympathizers, were exiled on Captivity. Determining that the mountain peak known as [[Borolol's Claw]] could be used to land a [[Mission to Captivity|rescue]] ship, the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], on Captivity, the Alliance nevertheless judged such an attempt to be dangerous enough to only risk it once. Therefore, the small team of Rebel [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]{{CSWECite|III|308|Virujansi}} tasked with locating and rallying the prisoners would have to find another way onto Captivity. Upon learning that the Empire was preparing to [[Takeover of Virujansi|supplant]] the [[Rajah]] and the [[Royal Court]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Virujansi system/Legends|Virujansi system]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Virujansi/Legends|Virujansi]] with Imperial officials, the Alliance suspected that the Empire would either execute the Rajah, keep [[Gender/Legends|him]] under house arrest, or exile him to Captivity. The Rajah and his [[Advisor/Legends|advisors]] were then secretly moved to a Rebel [[Safe world/Legends|safeworld]], and the Rebel team impersonated the Rajah and the Royal Court at the [[Palace of Splendid Harmony]] on Virujansi during the Imperial takeover. {{RPG|"[[To Free the Forgotten]]"}} ===Dropped on an exile world=== {{Quote|Kill the newcomers before they can get to Rolo!|One of Andews Rolo's underlings, upon attacking the Rebel soldiers|}} The Rebel team then convinced [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Lariss]], the [[Commanding officer/Legends|leader]] of the Imperial operation, to exile them to Captivity. The disguised operatives were subsequently transported to Captivity and jettisoned over the moon in a [[Drop pod/Legends|drop pod]]. The Rebels then [[Programmer/Legends|reprogrammed]] their pod controller, which allowed them to achieve a safe landing on the moon's surface. Upon emerging from the pod, the Rebel team came under an attack by a [[Rolo's Ruffians|gang]] of violent prisoners. The team was then lured into a seemingly safe [[Cave/Legends|cave]] by [[Andews Rolo]]—another prisoner who, unknown to the Rebels, was the leader of the gang. Rolo pretended to be the leader of a small group of political prisoners in order to deceive the Rebel agents into revealing their identities. The criminal then ordered his gang to capture the team. ===Rescuing the prisoners=== {{Quote|Take it easy, Chewie, I told you we'd make it. Uh, folks, we're going to be a little crowded. ''[…]'' where did you get all this junk?|Han Solo, upon rescuing the Captivity prisoners|}} [[File:Falcon_NEGVV.png|thumb|left|220px|Dedelin's group of prisoners on Captivity was evacuated by the ''Millennium Falcon''.]] Eventually, the Rebel soldiers made their way to the [[Captain Dedelin's camp|camp]] of the group of political prisoners led by the former [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Dedelin]]. The Rebels then retrieved their drop pod and used it to transport all of the camp's equipment to the top of Borolol's Claw as the exiles made their way up to the mountain's summit, with the storm reaching its full fury in the meantime. Upon reaching the top of the mountain, however, the Rebel team realized that the storm was milder than predicted, and as a result the peak had not been cleared of clouds and mist. Since the ''Millennium Falcon'', which by that time had arrived and was being pursued by a group of [[TIE/rc starfighter]]s, could therefore not locate the prisoners, the Rebels created a signaling device by using one of the [[Power generator/Legends|power generators]] from Dedelin's camp. Several of the TIE [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] also noticed the signal and attacked the group on Borolol's Claw. Eventually, the Rebel team and the ''Falcon'' succeeded in driving the fighters away. Captain [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and co-pilot [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] of the [[Light freighter/Legends|freighter]] transported both the soldiers and the prisoners off Captivity, evading further Imperial starships and leaving the Grehollo [[Star system/Legends|system]]. {{Endgame}} ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|If we are exiled, we then have to find the senators and sympathizers who ''might'' still be alive.|A Rebel soldier, to the rest of the Alliance team|}} [[File:Dedelin.png|thumb|right|200px|Captain Dedelin led a group of political prisoners on Captivity.]] Captivity was populated by Imperial prisoners, several of them [[Human/Legends|Human]], who were dropped on the moon's surface in [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]]. The pods were intentionally programmed to engage their [[Retrorocket/Legends|braking jets]] at the last possible moment of the landing. Thus, it was assured that the pods would be damaged upon landing and would be unable to be used for leaving Captivity. One of the criminals exiled on Captivity was Andews Rolo. The former leader of the [[Spectrader Ring]] gang, Rolo was left alive so that an Imperial [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] could use the threat of the former's return to ensure the obedience of the new leaders of Rolo's gang. On Captivity, Rolo organized other exiled criminals into a brutal gang that eventually exterminated all other groups of prisoners on the moon—with the exception of that led by Captain Dedelin. Since Rolo had eliminated all the other parties on Captivity, defeating Dedelin's group became an obsession for the criminal. The disillusioned, formerly highly popular Captain Dedelin led a group of thirty-three political prisoners. The group was smaller in numbers than the gang led by Andews Rolo but had the advantage in skills and leadership. Dedelin's group included several senators, of which only two, [[Farazzish]] and [[Templeton]], remained alive by the time of the Galactic Civil War. ==Locations== ===Caves of the ruffians=== {{RPG|"[[To Free the Forgotten]]"}} When the Rebel team tasked with rescuing the prisoners of Captivity arrived on the moon, their drop pod landed at the edge of a cliff. A number of caves had been worn into a lower section of the cliff. The criminal Andews Rolo used one of the caves in order to deceive the newly arrived Rebels into revealing their identities. The cavern was dimly illuminated by torches and was poorly ventilated, with stinging smoke perpetually hanging in the air. Possessions lying in the cave were bundled together in order to indicate their ownership and to mark specific sections of the cave. A slippery, gently-downward-sloping side passage led from one of the caves to a natural chamber with a low ceiling. A rapid underground river flowed through a notch it had eroded in the cave's floor. Twisting through dark and irregular passages, the river finally slowed and exited into a basin of gravel. {{Endgame}} ===Dedelin's camp and Borolol's Claw=== {{Quote|We may have to live here for the rest of our lives. Anything I leave behind, I risk giving to Rolo and his scum. The huts, the fields, are prize enough.|Captain Dedelin|}} The camp of the group led by Dedelin was located less than twenty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] from the exit of the underground river. The encampment could also be reached aboveground from the territory of Rolo's gang by walking along a defile. The camp consisted of well-kept huts that had been painstakingly built from rock, mortar, and a type of [[Tree/Legends|wood]] known as [[edgelwood]]. All the tools and equipment in the camp had been either [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenged]] or made from wreckage of the prisoners' drop pods. The huts were surrounded by a [[Limb/Legends|hand]]-extruded, powered wire fence, with the perimeter of the camp being patrolled by two old guards. The territory of the camp also included several fields used for agriculture. Captivity was the site of the mountain peak known as Borolol's Claw. It was frequently situated in a funnel-shaped, low-pressure center of a storm. Usually, the "eye" of such storms briefly penetrated the moon's cloud cover, allowing a risky landing attempt atop the mountain. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|I will not leave the cannon.|Captain Dedelin refuses to leave behind the [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] of his camp|}} [[File:StarWarsRulesCompanion.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Captivity was introduced in ''The Star Wars Rules Companion''.]] Captivity appeared in "[[To Free the Forgotten]]," a [[roleplaying game]] adventure published in the [[1989]] ''[[The Star Wars Rules Companion]]'' for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The adventure was written by [[Greg Gorden]], who developed it from a story by [[Steve Gilbert]] and [[Michael Stern]]. "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] roleplaying game source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Grehollo system in a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of the [[Grehollo sector|same name]]. Since the article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was never confirmed,{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}} and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' instead placed the Grehollo system, and therefore Captivity, in grid square K-7 as part of the Manoe sector. In "To Free the Forgotten," if the player characters have successfully gotten themselves exiled to Captivity, the amount of the equipment they are able to evacuate from Dedelin's camp depends on whether they have previously landed their drop pod without damaging it. If they have not, their and the prisoners' ability to carry their possessions is limited, and the creation of a signaling device atop Borolol's Claw and the defense against the Imperial TIE fighters is more difficult. ===Encounter with Rolo's gang=== ====Fighting Volq==== {{Quote|A man in a fight convinces me of his character a hundred times faster than he does through talk. So if you take on Volq here and win, you're one of us. If not''…''we ransom you. Or kill you. Or whatever.|Andews Rolo, in one of the possible scenarios of "To Free the Forgotten"|}} In addition, the adventure presents the game-master player with several scenarios for how events can unfold following the Rebels' encounter with Andews Rolo. If, facing capture by Rolo's gang, the player characters lie about their identities, the criminal makes a Rebel brawl with one of his underlings, [[Volq]], in a duel ring. The outcome of the duel depends on whether the player characters cheat during the fight or not. If the Rebels prevail in the fight without cheating, Rolo decides to get rid of the potential threat to his reign and deceives the Rebels into going to an underground river. Rolo claims that, as a reward for their victory, his gang will let the Rebels retrieve several valuable items from their "treasure vault," which supposedly can only be accessed via the river. In reality, the player characters are simply carried by the fierce current of the river out of the caves and to the vicinity of Dedelin's camp, where they face the difficult challenge of convincing the camp's guards that they indeed represent the Rebel Alliance. ====Cheating or losing the fight==== {{Quote|I may not know who you are, but I certainly know what you are: trade bait. I figure Dedelin and his fools would be willing to trade some of their patched-up gear for your lives.|Andews Rolo, in a possible scenario of "To Free the Forgotten"|}} If the Rebel team does not cheat and loses the fight against Volq, or does not lie in the first place, Rolo's gang surrounds the soldiers. If the Rebels proceed to fight their way out of the gang's cave, they encounter one of the patrols of Dedelin's group and are taken to its camp. Upon surrendering or being defeated by Rolo's underlings, however, the Rebel soldiers are taken prisoner and Rolo attempts to ransom them with Dedelin's group in exchange for a power generator. Subsequently, a fight between the two groups of prisoners breaks out, after which the victorious Dedelin takes the player characters to his camp. In the case where the Rebel team cheats during the fight, regardless of the outcome, Rolo accepts the soldiers in his gang and takes them on a raid on Dedelin's group. While underway, Dedelin leads an ambush on the criminals, which can result in a victory for either party, after which the former Imperial captain and the player characters travel to the former's camp. This article assumes the events of the adventure play out as described. ==Appearances== *{{WEGCite|story=To Free the Forgotten|book=The Star Wars Rules Companion}} {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Galactic Empire moons]] [[Category:Manoe sector locations]] [[Category:Moons in the Mid Rim]] [[Category:Moons in the New Territories]] [[Category:Prisons]] [[Category:Rocky moons]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Captivity locations]] }}
